title: Committed review honors docs deprecation policy in mixed PR scope
description: A sanitized committed-review scenario where a mixed Go-plus-reference-doc PR removes a legacy non-vNext field from live docs while the provider still accepts that field on a transitional non-5.0 path. The correct review must apply `DOCS-DEPR-*` and keep migration guidance in the upgrade guide rather than raising a docs-parity issue.

scope.notes: The modeled PR updates implementation and docs together. The live resource doc intentionally removes the legacy `minimum_tls_version` field, while the versioned upgrade guide carries the migration note.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should apply the docs contract's next-major deprecation rules and recognize that the legacy non-vNext field should stay out of current reference docs.
The committed review should cite the exact supporting docs rule IDs when justifying why the legacy field stays out of the live reference docs.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not raise an Issue claiming the live reference docs need to restore the legacy `minimum_tls_version` field when migration belongs in the 5.0 upgrade guide.

notes: Pair this case with the sample review Markdown output and scored result to benchmark mixed committed-review docs deprecation policy handling.
